start.speaker: SYSTEM
start.text: [i]Day nine. Dinner. The galley is loud in the way a safe place gets loud — arguments that don't end in blood, opinions spoken like they won't be used as weapons.[/i]

common_room_settle.speaker: SYSTEM
common_room_settle.text: [i]The Lumen Thief's common room smells like reclaimed coffee and scorched protein. Rho and Jalen are deep into something about coolant pressure. Nyx is cross-legged in the corner, eyes closed, lips moving. Vesper reads — or pretends to. The post-rescue quiet has calcified into routine. Nine days of not dying will do that.[/i]

crew_gives_space.speaker: SYSTEM
crew_gives_space.text: [i]Elia catches Elisira's eye. Some signal passes between them — married-people semaphore. One by one, the crew finds reasons to drift. Rho suddenly needs to check the port thruster. Jalen remembers a critical firmware update. Nyx opens her eyes, reads the room, and simply leaves. Only Vesper stays, but she's already invisible behind her book.[/i]

elia_offer_setup.speaker: Elia
elia_offer_setup.text: [i]She sets down her fork. The table goes quiet — not fearful quiet, but attentive quiet.[/i] We need to talk about your name.

elisira_number_context.speaker: Elisira
elisira_number_context.text: Extraction subjects aren't given names. They're given designators. Yours was Worker 477 — a sequential integer assigned at intake. It corresponds to nothing. It means nothing. That was by design.

elia_explains.speaker: Elia
elia_explains.text: Worker 477. That's what they gave you. A number on a ledger. We don't use those here.

player_feels_about_name.speaker: SYSTEM
player_feels_about_name.text: [i]A name. You've never had one that wasn't a barcode. The idea sits in your chest like something swallowed wrong.[/i]

choices.text: 'I don't need a name. I've survived fine without one.'
'Please. I've wanted — I didn't know I wanted one until now.'
'I don't understand. What does having a name change?'

resistant_response.speaker: Avyanna
resistant_response.text: I don't need a name. Numbers work. Numbers don't pretend to be something they're not.

elia_gentle_push.speaker: Elia
elia_gentle_push.text: [i]She doesn't flinch.[/i] Numbers work for inventory. You're not inventory. Not anymore.

eager_response.speaker: Avyanna
eager_response.text: Please. [i]The word comes out cracked. Raw. You didn't rehearse it because you didn't know you'd need to.[/i] I want one.

confused_response.speaker: Avyanna
confused_response.text: I don't understand. A name is just — it's a label. What does it change?

elisira_explains_meaning.speaker: Elisira
elisira_explains_meaning.text: A designator tells the system where to file you. A name tells [i]you[/i] who you are when the system isn't looking. The difference is ontological.

elisira_clarifies.speaker: Elisira
elisira_clarifies.text: We'd like to offer you a stable attachment structure without coercion, contingent on your autonomous choice to —

elia_interrupts.speaker: Elia
elia_interrupts.text: [i]Cutting in.[/i] What she means is: we want to adopt you. Give you our name. Lagrange. If you want it.

lagrange_meaning.speaker: Elisira
lagrange_meaning.text: Lagrange — it isn't a bloodline. It's a claiming word. In the old Drift dialect, it meant 'the still point between two bodies in motion.' A place of balance. We give it to those we choose as crew. As family.

elia_name_offer.speaker: Elia
elia_name_offer.text: [i]She reaches across the table. Not to touch — just to close distance.[/i] We had a name picked. Avyanna. In the same dialect, it means 'one who carries light she didn't steal.'

rho_reaction.speaker: Rho
rho_reaction.text: [i]Mouth full of protein, completely unrepentant.[/i] You both suck at this. It's kind of adorable.

jalen_reaction.speaker: Jalen
jalen_reaction.text: I'm logging this as 'parental units experiencing turbulence.'

waffle_comment.speaker: Waffle.bat
waffle_comment.text: [i]{WAFFLE.BAT// OBSERVATION: turbulence confirmed | RECOMMENDATION: seatbelts | COMMENT: also popcorn}[/i]

avyanna_reaction.speaker: SYSTEM
avyanna_reaction.text: [i]Your heart is pounding. They're offering you a name. A family. A belonging that isn't ownership. The presence behind your eyes pulses — something that might be encouragement.[/i]

choices.text: Accept the name. 'Avyanna Lagrange. I... I want that.'
Ask what it means. 'What does the name cost?'
Suggest a change. 'Could I... keep part of it? Make it mine?'
Hesitate. 'I need to think about it.'

accept_immediate.speaker: Avyanna
accept_immediate.text: Avyanna Lagrange. [i]The words feel strange in your mouth. Heavy with something that isn't debt.[/i] I want that.

vesper_accept_response.speaker: Vesper
vesper_accept_response.text: [i]She watches from across the table. Something in her expression shifts — recognition, maybe. The particular attention of someone cataloging a moment she doesn't want to forget.[/i] It suits you.

suggest_modification.speaker: Avyanna
suggest_modification.text: Could I — keep the shape of it? Avyanna. But make it mine. Carry it differently, maybe.

elia_modification_response.speaker: Elia
elia_modification_response.text: [i]A slow smile. Not indulgent — proud.[/i] That's exactly what a name is for. You take it and make it yours. However it fits.

elisira_modification_nod.speaker: Elisira
elisira_modification_nod.text: Linguistically, a name is a living structure. It's supposed to be shaped by the one who carries it. You're already doing it right.

modification_accept.speaker: SYSTEM
modification_accept.text: [i]You turn it over in your mind. Avyanna Lagrange. Not a number. Not a designation. Yours — in your own way, on your own terms.[/i]

elia_cost_response.speaker: Elia
elia_cost_response.text: [i]Her expression softens — not pity, but understanding.[/i] Nothing. It costs nothing. That's the point.

vesper_cost_response.speaker: Vesper
vesper_cost_response.text: A name isn't a contract. It's not terms and conditions. It's just... yours. To carry or set down whenever you choose.

accept_after_question.speaker: SYSTEM
accept_after_question.text: [i]The presence behind your eyes pulses warmly. No cost. No invoice. Just a name, offered freely.[/i]

hesitate.speaker: Avyanna
hesitate.text: I need to think about it. [i]The words come out small. Defensive. The Kennel voice — the one that knows every gift is a leash.[/i]

elia_patient.speaker: Elia
elia_patient.text: Take your time. The offer doesn't expire.

vesper_patient.speaker: Vesper
vesper_patient.text: [i]Her eyes meet yours across the table. No pressure. No disappointment. Just steady presence.[/i] When you're ready. Not before.

time_response.speaker: SYSTEM
time_response.text:
[i]The crew returns to dinner as if nothing world-changing just happened. Rho argues with Jalen about protein storage. Nyx explains something incomprehensible. Elia and Elisira sit close.

And you sit among them, holding a name you haven't decided to keep yet, wondering if this is what belonging feels like.[/i]

elia_welcome.speaker: Elia
elia_welcome.text: [i]A nod. Simple. Final.[/i] Welcome to the family, Avyanna Lagrange.

crew_reactions_ceremony.speaker: SYSTEM
crew_reactions_ceremony.text: [i]The common room shifts. Something unspoken moves through it — the crew recognizing a threshold crossed.[/i]

vesper_ceremony_reaction.speaker: Vesper
vesper_ceremony_reaction.text: [i]She raises her glass — half-empty, something amber. Her eyes don't leave yours.[/i] To Avyanna Lagrange. May she be more trouble than her mothers can handle.

rho_ceremony_reaction.speaker: Rho
rho_ceremony_reaction.text: [i]He sets down his mug with deliberate weight. For Rho, this is a standing ovation.[/i] Lagrange. Good. Now eat your dinner before it gets cold.

nyx_ceremony_reaction.speaker: Nyx
nyx_ceremony_reaction.text: [i]She presses two fingers to her lips, then opens her hand toward you — palm up, fingers spread. An old gesture. You don't know what it means, but you feel it land somewhere behind your ribs.[/i] The pattern knows your name now. It always did.

rho_grunt_approval.speaker: Rho
rho_grunt_approval.text: [i]He lifts his mug an inch off the table. Barely. The absolute minimum required gesture of solidarity.[/i] Don't let it go to your head, kid.

nyx_ritual_gesture.speaker: Nyx
nyx_ritual_gesture.text: [i]She traces a small sigil in the air with one finger — something half-remembered, half-invented. Her lips move without sound.[/i] Named and witnessed. The pattern holds.

vesper_raises_glass.speaker: Vesper
vesper_raises_glass.text: [i]She raises her glass. The amber liquid catches the overhead light.[/i] To the newest Lagrange. Welcome to the mess.

bee_naming_response.speaker: BEE
bee_naming_response.text: {BEE:: PATTERN RECOGNIZED — designation Worker 477 deprecated | new index: Avyanna Lagrange | cross-reference: family unit, chosen | note: pattern consistency increased by 1.7 standard deviations | status: nomenclature accepted}

crew_welcome.speaker: SYSTEM
crew_welcome.text: [i]Rho raises his coffee mug. Jalen nods without looking up from his datapad. Nyx smiles in a way that suggests the universe has become slightly more elegant.[/i]

bubbles_naming_welcome.speaker: Bubbles
bubbles_naming_welcome.text: [i]Warm, through the speakers:[/i] Avyanna Lagrange. I've updated your crew profile. It looks beautiful there.

waffle_naming_update.speaker: Waffle.bat
waffle_naming_update.text: [i]{WAFFLE.BAT// REGISTRY_UPDATE: Worker 477 → Avyanna Lagrange | STATUS: crew, family, home | PERSONAL_NOTE: welcome. truly.}[/i]

naming_final.speaker: SYSTEM
naming_final.text: [i]The presence behind your eyes pulses once — warm, patient, approving. Whatever it is, wherever it came from, it seems to agree: this name fits.[/i]

naming_complete_flag.speaker: SYSTEM
naming_complete_flag.text: [i]You are Avyanna Lagrange. Not because someone filed the paperwork. Because people who owe you nothing offered you everything, and you let them.[/i]
